days.title: Kafka Architecture
learningObjectives: Explain Kafka Architecture with phase-7 depth and clarity
Implement Java examples for Kafka Architecture with production awareness
Identify failure risks and mitigation strategies
Reason about trade-offs under realistic constraints
Design or optimize solutions with maintainability in mind
Present interview answers with concrete evidence and structure

sections.title: Why Kafka Architecture matters
sections.content:
Kafka Architecture is a high-value Day 59 topic in Phase 7 (Kafka and messaging reliability at scale).

At this stage, interviewers evaluate whether you can connect implementation details with reliability, scalability, and maintainability outcomes.

What strong answers include:
1. precise concept framing,
2. operational implications,
3. one realistic failure pattern,
4. one practical mitigation.

This topic is not just about code that compiles. It is about decisions that survive production traffic and team ownership.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Kafka Architecture
sections.content:
### Day 59: Kafka Architecture
Phase context: Kafka and messaging reliability at scale.

### What to understand deeply
- mechanism and boundaries
- runtime behavior and cost
- operational failure modes
- trade-offs vs alternatives

### Implementation workflow
1. Build a clear baseline solution.
2. Validate edge/failure paths.
3. Measure impact (latency, memory, throughput, or complexity).
4. Refactor for readability and maintainability.

### Interview framing template
- "The problem this solves is..."
- "I chose this approach because..."
- "The key trade-off is..."
- "A failure case is... and mitigation is..."

### Production perspective
For Kafka Architecture, quality is judged by correctness + resilience + debuggability, not just API usage.

sections.title: Basic Walkthrough
sections.description: Kafka topic = ordered log; partition = unit of parallelism.

sections.title: Intermediate Implementation
sections.description: Key-based partition: same key goes to same partition for ordering.

sections.title: Advanced Production Pattern
sections.description: Consumer group: partitions assigned across members — rebalance on join/leave.

sections.title: Architecture View - Day 59
sections.description: Visual model for Kafka Architecture

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
items: Choosing partition keys that break ordering expectations.
Incorrect offset commit strategy leading to loss/duplicates.
No dead-letter policy for poison messages.
Assuming exactly-once semantics without full transactional flow.
Missing lag and consumer-health observability.

sections.title: Exercise - Kafka Architecture Applied Scenario
sections.problem:
Implement a realistic solution for **Kafka Architecture**.

Requirements:
1. Define assumptions and constraints first.
2. Include one failure-path handling strategy.
3. Provide one improvement over baseline.
4. State complexity or runtime impact.
5. Keep implementation review-friendly.
hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is Kafka topic?
conceptual.answer: Category feed of messages—immutable log segments.

conceptual.question: What is partition?
conceptual.answer: Ordered immutable sequence within topic—unit of parallelism.

conceptual.question: What is broker?
conceptual.answer: Kafka server storing partitions—cluster of brokers.

conceptual.question: What is replication factor?
conceptual.answer: Copies per partition—ISR in-sync replicas leader follower.

conceptual.question: What is consumer group?
conceptual.answer: Set of consumers dividing partitions—scale consumption horizontally.

conceptual.question: What is offset?
conceptual.answer: Position in partition log—committed offset tracks progress.

conceptual.question: What is retention policy?
conceptual.answer: Time or size based retention—compaction for changelog topics.

conceptual.question: What is key in Kafka record?
conceptual.answer: Same key goes to same partition—ordering per key.

conceptual.question: What is ZooKeeper vs KRaft?
conceptual.answer: Legacy metadata quorum vs Kafka Raft mode—new clusters KRaft.

conceptual.question: What is log compaction?
conceptual.answer: Retain latest key value—changelog semantics—tombstone deletes.

conceptual.question: What is exactly-once in Kafka?
conceptual.answer: Idempotent producer transactions consumer isolation—EOS semantics.

conceptual.question: What is consumer lag?
conceptual.answer: Difference high-water mark and committed offset—backlog metric.

conceptual.question: What is rebalance?
conceptual.answer: Consumer group membership change redistributes partitions—stop-the-world impact.

conceptual.question: What is idempotent producer enable.idempotence?
conceptual.answer: Deduplicate retries PID sequence numbers.

conceptual.question: What is transactions in Kafka?
conceptual.answer: Atomic write multiple partitions—consume-transform-produce pattern.

codeBased.question: subscribe(List topics) vs assign partitions manual

seniorScenario.question: Broker disk full cluster halt?
seniorScenario.answer: Monitor disk retention sizing alerts tiered storage.

seniorScenario.question: Rebalance storm during deploy?
seniorScenario.answer: Cooperative sticky assignors tuning session timeouts.

seniorScenario.question: Ordering guarantee cross partition?
seniorScenario.answer: Not global—design keys carefully single partition hot spots.

seniorScenario.question: Kafka as database anti-pattern?
seniorScenario.answer: Operational complexity; use proper DB for query patterns.

seniorScenario.question: Multi-region Kafka MirrorMaker?
seniorScenario.answer: Latency offset lag disaster recovery complexity.

seniorScenario.question: PII in topics GDPR?
seniorScenario.answer: Encryption masking access control retention minimization.

wrongAnswers: Kafka is a traditional message queue deleting messages after consume.
A topic always has exactly one partition.
Consumer groups allow multiple consumers to read the same partition in parallel.
Kafka brokers store messages in PostgreSQL.
Offsets are managed only by the broker, never by clients.
Kafka guarantees global ordering across all partitions in a topic.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Area | Phase 7 Expectation | Quick Rule | Gotcha |
|---|---|---|---|
| Framing | Explain intent + constraints | Start from problem | API-only answer |
| Design | State trade-off explicitly | Compare alternatives | One-solution bias |
| Runtime | Mention cost impact | Time + space/ops | No performance reasoning |
| Failure | Name one incident pattern | Mitigation path | Happy-path only thinking |
| Communication | Be concise + concrete | Use one example | Generic buzzwords |

hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is Kafka producer batching?
conceptual.answer: linger.ms batch.size accumulate records throughput vs latency trade-off.

conceptual.question: What is compression?
conceptual.answer: lz4 snappy gzip—CPU vs network trade-off.

conceptual.question: What is acks configuration?
conceptual.answer: 0 fire-and-forget, 1 leader ack, all ISR ack—durability trade-off.

conceptual.question: What is retries and ordering?
conceptual.answer: With max.in.flight >1 without idempotence reorder risk—tune carefully.

conceptual.question: What is sticky partitioner?
conceptual.answer: Better batching same partition until batch full—throughput.

conceptual.question: What is buffer memory?
conceptual.answer: Producer buffer before send—block or fail when full.

conceptual.question: What is max.block.ms?
conceptual.answer: How long metadata fetch buffer allocation block—latency impact.

conceptual.question: What is delivery timeout?
conceptual.answer: Upper bound send attempt including retries—align with broker settings.

conceptual.question: What is transactional producer?
conceptual.answer: Exactly-once produce multiple partitions atomic—overhead.

conceptual.question: What is schema evolution with producer?
conceptual.answer: Compatible serializers—fail fast on mismatch.

conceptual.question: What is partitioner customization?
conceptual.answer: Custom routing beyond key hash—business logic.

conceptual.question: What is record headers?
conceptual.answer: Metadata per message—tracing propagation without payload pollution.

conceptual.question: What is idempotent producer sequence numbers?
conceptual.answer: PID epoch per producer instance—broker dedupe.

conceptual.question: What is linger trade-off?
conceptual.answer: Higher linger better throughput worse latency tail.

conceptual.question: What is client DNS cache refresh?
conceptual.answer: Broker discovery changes—rare but painful incidents.

seniorScenario.question: Producer throughput collapse latency spike?
seniorScenario.answer: Batch too small? Network partition? Broker hot partition? Profile.

seniorScenario.question: Duplicate events after network blip?
seniorScenario.answer: Idempotent producer and consumer dedupe keys.

seniorScenario.question: Ordered delivery violated?
seniorScenario.answer: Retries in flight settings idempotence enable—verify config.

seniorScenario.question: Memory leak producer buffer?
seniorScenario.answer: Close producers on shutdown; avoid per-request new producer.

seniorScenario.question: Schema registry down producer fail?
seniorScenario.answer: Cache schemas fallback strategy degrade gracefully.

seniorScenario.question: Cross-datacenter produce latency?
seniorScenario.answer: Local produce to mirror—architecture pattern.

wrongAnswers: acks=all means the producer waits for all brokers in the cluster including observers.
linger.ms=0 always minimizes end-to-end latency in every scenario.
Producers should create a new KafkaProducer per message for isolation.
Compression never increases CPU usage.
Transactional producers are free and have no performance impact.
max.in.flight can be unlimited without risk.

sections.title: Basic Walkthrough
sections.description: Consumer offset: commit after processing to avoid data loss on crash.

sections.title: Intermediate Implementation
sections.description: At-least-once: duplicate delivery possible — make handler idempotent.

sections.title: Advanced Production Pattern
sections.description: Consumer lag: high-water offset minus current position.

Implement a realistic solution for **Kafka Consumers Deep Dive**.

1. Define assumptions and constraints first.
2. Include one failure-path handling strategy.

4. State complexity or runtime impact.
5. Keep implementation review-friendly.
hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is consumer poll loop?
conceptual.answer: Long-polling fetch records batch processing commit offsets.

conceptual.question: What is auto commit vs manual?
conceptual.answer: enable.auto.commit periodic vs commitSync after processing—at-least-once vs control.

conceptual.question: What is consumer group rebalance protocol?
conceptual.answer: Assign partitions when consumers join leave—EAGER vs COOPERATIVE.

conceptual.question: What is static membership group.instance.id?
conceptual.answer: Reduce unnecessary rebalance on transient restarts—sticky identity.

conceptual.question: What is fetch.min.bytes max.wait.ms?
conceptual.answer: Consumer batching trade-off latency throughput.

conceptual.question: What is isolation.level read_committed?
conceptual.answer: Only read transactional commits—EOS consumers.

conceptual.question: What is max.poll.interval.ms?
conceptual.answer: If processing too long between polls—consider pause partitions async processing.

conceptual.question: What is heartbeats session.timeout.ms?
conceptual.answer: Detect failed consumers—tune with GC pauses mind.

conceptual.question: What is assign vs subscribe?
conceptual.answer: Manual partition assignment vs dynamic group—advanced.

conceptual.question: What is incremental cooperative rebalancing?
conceptual.answer: Reduce stop-the-world during rebalance—Kafka 2.4+.

conceptual.question: What is consumer lag monitoring?
conceptual.answer: Critical SLO—alert thresholds scale consumers.

conceptual.question: What is idempotent consumer?
conceptual.answer: Processing twice safe—business keys idempotency store.

conceptual.question: What is pause/resume partitions?
conceptual.answer: Backpressure slow processing pipeline—stop fetching temporarily.

conceptual.question: What is deserialization error handler?
conceptual.answer: DLQ poison messages—skip fail strategies.

conceptual.question: What is exactly-once consumer?
conceptual.answer: Read process write transactional outbox EOS—complex.

codeBased.question: AckMode MANUAL_IMMEDIATE
codeBased.answer: Commit after each record Spring Kafka.

seniorScenario.question: Consumer lag growing unbounded—ops?
seniorScenario.answer: Scale consumers partitions enough poison messages stuck.

seniorScenario.question: Rebalance loop crash repeating?
seniorScenario.answer: Processing exceeds max.poll.interval—async processing pause pattern.

seniorScenario.question: Duplicate processing money movement?
seniorScenario.answer: Idempotency keys ledger—detect replay.

seniorScenario.question: Consumer reading old messages after deploy?
seniorScenario.answer: Offset reset misconfiguration auto.offset.reset.

seniorScenario.question: Multi-tenant noisy neighbor partition hot?
seniorScenario.answer: Key skew monitoring rebalance keys.

seniorScenario.question: GDPR erase events consumed already?
seniorScenario.answer: Compensating delete downstream systems—hard.

wrongAnswers: Consumers pull messages from Kafka; Kafka pushes to consumers continuously without polling.
enable.auto.commit guarantees exactly-once processing.
Increasing max.poll.interval.ms fixes all slow consumer problems without design changes.
Consumer group id is optional and ignored.
Manual commit always means at-most-once semantics.
Rebalances never pause consumption.

sections.title: Intermediate Implementation
sections.description: Transactional id + epoch for EOS with Kafka (conceptual fields).

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is at-most-once delivery?
conceptual.answer: Messages may be lost—fire and forget—rarely acceptable for money.

conceptual.question: What is at-least-once delivery?
conceptual.answer: Duplicates possible—consumers must dedupe—common default.

conceptual.question: What is exactly-once semantics end-to-end?
conceptual.answer: Composite: idempotent producers, transactions, idempotent consumers—hard.

conceptual.question: What is Kafka EOS transaction?
conceptual.answer: Producer initTransactions begin commit atomic multi partition.

conceptual.question: What is read_committed consumer?
conceptual.answer: Skips open transactions aborted—sees only committed transactional messages.

conceptual.question: What is zombie fencing?
conceptual.answer: Transactional id epoch prevents old producer instances writing—fence older zombies.

conceptual.question: What is outbox pattern relation?
conceptual.answer: Local DB transaction + event ensures atomicity with business row—EOS illusion.

conceptual.question: What is idempotent consumer dedupe store?
conceptual.answer: Unique constraint on message id processed flag.

conceptual.question: What is log compaction EOS?
conceptual.answer: Compacted topics still need ordering idempotence—careful.

conceptual.question: What is end-to-end latency vs EOS overhead?
conceptual.answer: Transactions add latency coordination—measure.

conceptual.question: What is duplicate detection window?
conceptual.answer: Time bounded dedupe store size—TTL old keys.

conceptual.question: What is JMS exactly-once limitations?
conceptual.answer: Different model—Kafka EOS more explicit modern.

conceptual.question: What is cloud provider messaging guarantees?
conceptual.answer: SQS at-least-once typically—design idempotency always.

conceptual.question: What is financial ledger double-entry with events?
conceptual.answer: Immutable append-only compensations—audit friendly.

conceptual.question: What is testing EOS?
conceptual.answer: Chaos duplicate delivery simulation property tests.

codeBased.question: producer initTransactions beginTransaction send partitions commitTransaction
insert into processed(id) values(?) on conflict do nothing
idempotency key HTTP header store response replay
Retry with same idempotent producer PID
Unit test duplicate delivery twice assert once effect

seniorScenario.question: Finance claims “exactly-once” marketing—engineering response?
seniorScenario.answer: Clarify boundaries end-to-end guarantees operational dedupe.

seniorScenario.question: EOS overhead unacceptable latency SLA?
seniorScenario.answer: Maybe at-least-once + strong idempotency cheaper.

seniorScenario.question: Cross-system EOS database + Kafka?
seniorScenario.answer: Outbox inbox patterns distributed transaction alternative 2PC.

seniorScenario.question: Bug duplicate charges—postmortem?
seniorScenario.answer: Missing idempotency keys replay storm—fix dedupe monitoring.

seniorScenario.question: Zombie instance cloud autoscaler?
seniorScenario.answer: Epoch fencing verification deployment uniqueness.

seniorScenario.question: Regulatory audit prove no duplicates?
seniorScenario.answer: Immutable logs reconciliation reports signatures.

wrongAnswers: Kafka exactly-once means you never need idempotent consumers.
At-most-once is the safest default for payments.
Transactions in Kafka remove all performance costs.
read_uncommitted is required for exactly-once.
Idempotency keys can be reused forever without storage growth concerns.
EOS guarantees exactly-once across external HTTP calls automatically.

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
items: Choosing partition keys that break ordering expectations.
Incorrect offset commit strategy leading to loss/duplicates.
No dead-letter policy for poison messages.
Assuming exactly-once semantics without full transactional flow.
Missing lag and consumer-health observability.

sections.title: Exercise - Kafka Streams Applied Scenario
sections.problem:
Implement a realistic solution for **Kafka Streams**.

Requirements:
1. Define assumptions and constraints first.
2. Include one failure-path handling strategy.
3. Provide one improvement over baseline.
4. State complexity or runtime impact.
5. Keep implementation review-friendly.
hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is Kafka Streams?
conceptual.answer: Library for stream processing on top of Kafka—state stores interactive queries.

conceptual.question: What is KStream vs KTable?
conceptual.answer: Record stream vs changelog table—dual semantics important.

conceptual.question: What is stateful processing?
conceptual.answer: Aggregates joins need RocksDB state stores changelog topics.

conceptual.question: What is windowing?
conceptual.answer: Tumbling hopping sliding time windows—event time processing.

conceptual.question: What is punctuator?
conceptual.answer: Periodic processing wall-clock time—watermarks advanced.

conceptual.question: What is repartition topic?
conceptual.answer: Kafka Streams internal repartition for key changes—hidden topics.

conceptual.question: What is exactly-once in Streams?
conceptual.answer: processing.guarantee exactly_once_v2—EOS config.

conceptual.question: What is interactive queries?
conceptual.answer: Query local state stores from app—embedding serving layer.

conceptual.question: What is changelog compaction state?
conceptual.answer: Restore state on restart—size retention tuning.

conceptual.question: What is join stream-stream windowed?
conceptual.answer: Both sides within window—time synchronization critical.

conceptual.question: What is processor API lower level?
conceptual.answer: Transform punctuate custom—advanced.

conceptual.question: What is DSL vs Processor API trade-off?
conceptual.answer: DSL concise; Processor flexible harder test.

conceptual.question: What is standby replicas?
conceptual.answer: Kafka Streams fault tolerance—state sync.

conceptual.question: What is consumer lag in streams tasks?
conceptual.answer: Task stuck processing poison—dead-letter strategies.

conceptual.question: What is migration from batch ETL?
conceptual.answer: Incremental stream processing lower latency—operational shift.

seniorScenario.question: RocksDB disk full streams crash?
seniorScenario.answer: Monitor disk state store size retention changelog compaction.

seniorScenario.question: Reprocessing historical data streams job?
seniorScenario.answer: Reset offsets carefully duplicate side effects idempotent sinks.

seniorScenario.question: Event time vs processing time skew?
seniorScenario.answer: Late data windows incomplete—watermark strategies.

seniorScenario.question: Interactive queries consistency?
seniorScenario.answer: Eventually consistent across instances—cache coherence issues.

seniorScenario.question: Upgrade Kafka Streams version incompatible state?
seniorScenario.answer: Migration tooling backup plan—risky.

seniorScenario.question: Complex join business logic in streams vs external DB?
seniorScenario.answer: Maintainability trade-off team skills.

wrongAnswers: Kafka Streams requires a separate Spark cluster.
KTable and KStream are identical concepts.
State stores are stored only in memory never on disk.
Kafka Streams cannot join streams.
Windowing is only possible with processing time.
Exactly-once in Streams requires no configuration.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is Schema Registry?
conceptual.answer: Central store for Avro/Protobuf/JSON schemas with compatibility rules.

conceptual.question: What is subject naming strategies?
conceptual.answer: TopicNameStrategy RecordNameStrategy TopicRecordNameStrategy—field evolution impact.

conceptual.question: What is backward compatibility?
conceptual.answer: New schema can read data written by old schema—consumers upgrade first typical.

conceptual.question: What is forward compatibility?
conceptual.answer: Old schema can read data written by new—less common pattern.

conceptual.question: What is full compatibility?
conceptual.answer: Both directions—strictest.

conceptual.question: What is Kafka Connect?
conceptual.answer: Framework connectors source/sink systems—configurable scalable.

conceptual.question: What is SMT single message transforms?
conceptual.answer: Tweak records in flight—mask fields rename—careful complexity.

conceptual.question: What is Debezium CDC connector?
conceptual.answer: Stream DB changes to Kafka—outbox alternative capture.

conceptual.question: What is dead letter queue in Connect?
conceptual.answer: Failed records route DLQ—replay tooling.

conceptual.question: What is connector task parallelism?
conceptual.answer: Tasks scale partitions—ordering per partition preserved.

conceptual.question: What is schema id in wire format?
conceptual.answer: Confluent wire format 0x0 magic byte schema id payload—efficient registry lookup.

conceptual.question: What is protobuf vs avro in registry?
conceptual.answer: Both supported—tooling differences codegen.

conceptual.question: What is connector offset management?
conceptual.answer: Exactly-once sink tricky—idempotent writes upsert.

conceptual.question: What is self-managed registry HA?
conceptual.answer: Clustered registry deployment backup—critical dependency.

conceptual.question: What is governance approval workflow?
conceptual.answer: Schema evolution PR reviews automated compatibility tests CI.

seniorScenario.question: Registry outage producers fail?
seniorScenario.answer: Cache schemas local resilience modes degrade carefully.

seniorScenario.question: Breaking schema deployed production?
seniorScenario.answer: Rollback consumers producers coordination freeze fixes.

seniorScenario.question: PII in schema evolution masking?
seniorScenario.answer: SMT redaction field-level security—compliance.

seniorScenario.question: Connect connector zombie tasks?
seniorScenario.answer: Monitoring task failure restart backoff stuck.

seniorScenario.question: Multi-tenant schema namespaces?
seniorScenario.answer: Subject naming isolation governance policies.

seniorScenario.question: Cost of registry HA infrastructure?
seniorScenario.answer: Business critical dependency—SRE ownership.

wrongAnswers: Schema Registry is optional because JSON is self-describing enough.
Backward compatibility means old consumers can read data produced by new schemas in all cases without rules.
Kafka Connect only supports reading from Kafka, not writing to databases.
Avro and JSON Schema cannot be stored in the same registry.
SMTs should contain all business logic.
Schema IDs are random UUIDs on every message.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is Spring Kafka?
conceptual.answer: Spring integration KafkaTemplate listeners container factories—idiomatic Spring.

conceptual.question: What is KafkaTemplate send?
conceptual.answer: Producer wrapper with sync async ListenableFuture.

conceptual.question: What is @KafkaListener?
conceptual.answer: Annotation-driven consumer method—container underneath.

conceptual.question: What is ConcurrentMessageListenerContainer?
conceptual.answer: Multiple consumer threads—scale within JVM.

conceptual.question: What is ChainedKafkaTransactionManager legacy?
conceptual.answer: Kafka + DB transaction bridging—deprecated patterns careful.

conceptual.question: What is @Transactional Kafka producer spring?
conceptual.answer: KafkaTransactionManager synchronize with DB—advanced EOS.

conceptual.question: What is DeadLetterPublishingRecoverer?
conceptual.answer: Send failed records to DLT topic—Spring Retry.

conceptual.question: What is ErrorHandler vs DefaultErrorHandler?
conceptual.answer: Seek behavior classification recoverable fatal—Spring Kafka 2.8+.

conceptual.question: What is RecordInterceptor?
conceptual.answer: Mutate records before listener—headers enrichment.

conceptual.question: What is batch listener?
conceptual.answer: Receive List ConsumerRecords—higher throughput careful commits.

conceptual.question: What is Micrometer Kafka metrics?
conceptual.answer: Spring Boot auto metrics—bind to Prometheus.

conceptual.question: What is EmbeddedKafka for tests?
conceptual.answer: In-memory broker tests—Testcontainers alternative more realistic.

conceptual.question: What is KafkaAdmin bean?
conceptual.answer: Declarative topic creation NewTopic beans.

conceptual.question: What is ProducerListener callback?
conceptual.answer: Observe success failure send metrics.

conceptual.question: What is spring.kafka.ssl configuration?
conceptual.answer: Security protocol SASL_SSL truststore keystore production.

seniorScenario.question: Spring Kafka upgrade breaking ErrorHandler behavior?
seniorScenario.answer: Read migration guide seek semantics classification.

seniorScenario.question: Transactional Kafka + DB same transaction pitfalls?
seniorScenario.answer: XA rare; outbox often simpler—understand boundaries.

seniorScenario.question: Listener throws exception infinite retry loop?
seniorScenario.answer: Backoff max attempts DLQ alert ops.

seniorScenario.question: High lag container paused processing bug?
seniorScenario.answer: Thread pool saturation backpressure design.

seniorScenario.question: Serialization poison message stuck partition?
seniorScenario.answer: Skip to DLQ with classifier monitoring.

seniorScenario.question: Multi-cluster Kafka Spring config?
seniorScenario.answer: Multiple factories templates—complexity isolation.

wrongAnswers: @KafkaListener works without a ConsumerFactory bean.
Spring Kafka guarantees exactly-once end-to-end with default settings.
KafkaTemplate only supports synchronous sends.
EmbeddedKafka is recommended for production smoke tests in clusters.
DeadLetterPublishingRecoverer always deletes the original message.
Spring Kafka replaces the need for native Kafka clients entirely.

sections.problem:
Implement a realistic solution for **RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging**.

Requirements:
1. Define assumptions and constraints first.
2. Include one failure-path handling strategy.

hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is RabbitMQ model?
conceptual.answer: Producers publish to exchanges; routes to queues via bindings; consumers pull ack.

conceptual.question: What is AMQP 0-9-1?
conceptual.answer: Protocol RabbitMQ implements—queues durable exclusive auto-delete properties.

conceptual.question: What is exchange types direct topic fanout headers?
conceptual.answer: Routing strategies—topic wildcards useful.

conceptual.question: What is acknowledgment modes?
conceptual.answer: Auto ack vs manual—reliability trade-off message loss vs duplicate.

conceptual.question: What is prefetch count?
conceptual.answer: Consumer prefetch limits unacked messages—backpressure fairness.

conceptual.question: What is dead letter exchange?
conceptual.answer: Route failed messages after retries—inspection requeue.

conceptual.question: What is TTL on messages or queues?
conceptual.answer: Expire messages—time-sensitive tasks.

conceptual.question: What is priority queue?
conceptual.answer: Priority property limited support—ordering caveats.

conceptual.question: What is quorum queues RabbitMQ 3.8+?
conceptual.answer: Replicated queues safer—classic mirrored queues legacy.

conceptual.question: What is AWS SQS?
conceptual.answer: Managed queue service—visibility timeout at-least-once—different semantics than Rabbit.

conceptual.question: What is SNS fan-out?
conceptual.answer: Pub/sub push to many SQS—integration pattern.

conceptual.question: What is FIFO SQS?
conceptual.answer: Ordering dedupe—throughput limits—exactly-once-ish with dedupe window.

conceptual.question: What is Kinesis vs Kafka?
conceptual.answer: Managed streams AWS—different operational model.

conceptual.question: What is message size limits?
conceptual.answer: SQS 256KB Rabbit large messages disk—design chunking reference storage.

conceptual.question: What is cross-cloud messaging?
conceptual.answer: VPN private links security costs—architecture constraints.

seniorScenario.question: Rabbit cluster split brain messages duplicated?
seniorScenario.answer: Design idempotent consumers federation carefully.

seniorScenario.question: SQS poison message loop?
seniorScenario.answer: DLQ maxReceiveCount alarm replay tooling.

seniorScenario.question: Cost SQS at scale?
seniorScenario.answer: Per request pricing batching long poll efficiency.

seniorScenario.question: On-prem Rabbit to cloud migration?
seniorScenario.answer: Dual write bridge gradual cutover monitoring.

seniorScenario.question: Ordering guarantees Rabbit single queue?
seniorScenario.answer: Single consumer ordering; multiple consumers interleaved.

seniorScenario.question: Compliance message encryption KMS?
seniorScenario.answer: SQS SSE KMS policies rotation.

wrongAnswers: RabbitMQ and Kafka are identical log-based systems.
SQS guarantees exactly-once delivery always.
Auto acknowledgment is the safest choice for payments.
FIFO SQS has unlimited throughput.
AMQP and MQTT are the same protocol.
Dead letter queues automatically fix bad messages.

sections.problem:
Implement a realistic solution for **Distributed Systems Theory**.

Requirements:
1. Define assumptions and constraints first.

3. Provide one improvement over baseline.

hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is CAP theorem?
conceptual.answer: Consistency Availability Partition tolerance—during partition must choose CP or AP trade-off.

conceptual.question: What is linearizability?
conceptual.answer: Strong consistency model operations appear atomic instant—hard distributed.

conceptual.question: What is eventual consistency?
conceptual.answer: Replicas converge later—acceptable many systems with UX handling staleness.

conceptual.question: What is quorum reads writes?
conceptual.answer: R+W > N ensures overlap—tunable consistency latency.

conceptual.question: What is vector clocks?
conceptual.answer: Detect concurrent updates causal ordering—Dynamo style.

conceptual.question: What is Lamport logical clocks?
conceptual.answer: Partial ordering events without physical clock sync.

conceptual.question: What is FLP impossibility?
conceptual.answer: No deterministic consensus in async network with one faulty process—foundational.

conceptual.question: What is Paxos Raft?
conceptual.answer: Consensus algorithms leader election log replication—etcd Raft.

conceptual.question: What is Byzantine failures?
conceptual.answer: Malicious nodes—blockchain concerns—different from crash faults.

conceptual.question: What is gossip protocol?
conceptual.answer: Epidemic dissemination membership info—scalable but eventual.

conceptual.question: What is split brain cluster?
conceptual.answer: Two partitions both think primary—data divergence dangerous—fencing tokens.

conceptual.question: What is lease vs lock?
conceptual.answer: Time-bounded lock reduce stuck forever—clock skew risks.

conceptual.question: What is hinted handoff?
conceptual.answer: Temporary responsibility during failures—Dynamo technique.

conceptual.question: What is read repair?
conceptual.answer: Read triggers reconciliation replicas—anti-entropy.

conceptual.question: What is Merkle trees?
conceptual.answer: Compare replica data efficiently—Cassandra repair.

codeBased.question: Zookeeper ephemeral sequential nodes leader election
Vector clock merge function
codeBased.answer: Pick max per node increment own.

seniorScenario.question: Outage split brain two primaries writes?
seniorScenario.answer: STONITH fencing durable storage epoch tokens—operations nightmare.

seniorScenario.question: “Strong consistency” marketing vs reality?
seniorScenario.answer: Clarify model linearizable serializable session causal—precise language.

seniorScenario.question: Global clock sync across regions impossible?
seniorScenario.answer: TrueTime bounded uncertainty engineering trade-offs.

seniorScenario.question: Blockchain for internal ledger hype?
seniorScenario.answer: Usually overkill CRDT or SQL sufficient—cost.

seniorScenario.question: Testing distributed systems failures?
seniorScenario.answer: Jepsen chaos frameworks partition injection.

seniorScenario.question: SLA 99.999% requires formal methods?
seniorScenario.answer: Often extensive testing redundancy—not only math.

wrongAnswers: CAP says you can have all three in practice always.
Eventual consistency means data is never consistent.
Raft and Paxos are unrelated to distributed consensus.
Vector clocks give total order of all events in the system automatically.
Byzantine fault tolerance is required for all cloud databases.
Quorum reads always guarantee the latest write immediately.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Area | Phase 7 Expectation | Quick Rule | Gotcha |
|---|---|---|---|
| Framing | Explain intent + constraints | Start from problem | API-only answer |
| Design | State trade-off explicitly | Compare alternatives | One-solution bias |
| Runtime | Mention cost impact | Time + space/ops | No performance reasoning |
| Failure | Name one incident pattern | Mitigation path | Happy-path only thinking |
| Communication | Be concise + concrete | Use one example | Generic buzzwords |
